name: Innovation Actions
summary: Innovation Actions (IA) are one of the core action types in [[Horizon Europe]], designed to fund activities directly aimed at producing plans and arrangements or designs for new, altered, or improved products, processes, or services. They cover prototyping, testing, demonstrating, piloting, large-scale product validation, and market replication.
body:
# Overview

Innovation Actions (IA) are one of the core action types in [[Horizon Europe]], designed to fund activities directly aimed at producing plans and arrangements or designs for new, altered, or improved products, processes, or services. They cover prototyping, testing, demonstrating, piloting, large-scale product validation, and market replication.

# Funding Rate

Innovation Actions are funded at **70% of eligible costs** (100% for non-profit legal entities). This lower co-funding rate compared to Research and Innovation Actions (100%) reflects the closer-to-market nature of IAs and the expectation that participants contribute more of their own resources.

# Key Characteristics

- Evaluated on Excellence, Impact, and Quality and Efficiency of Implementation
- Stronger emphasis on exploitation, market readiness, and business models compared to RIAs
- [[Dissemination and Exploitation]] obligations are reinforced, including the requirement to use the Horizon Results Platform if results are unexploited after one year
- Intellectual property management strategy must be commensurate with desired outcomes and impacts

# China Exclusion

Legal entities established in China are **not eligible to participate in Horizon Europe Innovation Actions in any capacity**. This includes participation as beneficiaries, affiliated entities, associated partners, third parties giving in-kind contributions, subcontractors, or recipients of financial support to third parties. Exceptions may be granted on a case-by-case basis for justified reasons. The exclusion is justified under Article 22(6) of the HE Regulation, given concerns regarding intellectual property generated under publicly funded programmes and the ongoing EU-China Joint Roadmap discussions.

# Participation Restrictions

Beyond the China exclusion, Innovation Actions are more likely to carry additional participation restrictions:
- Actions related to EU strategic assets, interests, autonomy, or security may limit participation to EU Member States and specific associated countries only (Article 22(5))
- Entities controlled directly or indirectly by ineligible third countries may be excluded
- Additional exploitation obligations may apply for up to four years after the end of the action, linked to restrictions of participation or control due to [[Open Strategic Autonomy]] considerations

# Evaluation Context

Innovation Actions and Programme Co-fund Actions are evaluated under the *Excellence* criterion with consideration of the [[Gender Equality|gender dimension]] and [[Open Science]] practices under methodology. The evaluation template for IAs reflects the closer-to-market positioning with greater weight on impact pathways and exploitation credibility.

# Connections

- [[Horizon Europe]] — core action type across all pillars
- [[International Cooperation]] — China exclusion, participation restrictions
- [[Open Strategic Autonomy]] — strategic exploitation obligations
- [[Dissemination and Exploitation]] — reinforced exploitation requirements
- [[Innovation Procurement]] — complementary demand-side mechanism
- [[European Innovation Council]] — EIC Accelerator supports IA-type activities
- [[Lump Sum Funding]] — may apply to IAs below EUR 10 million
- [[Clean Industrial Deal]] — horizontal call includes IA-type 'fit-for-deployment' projects

# Implications for Proposal Design

- Budget at 70% funding rate requires co-financing strategy
- Exploitation plan and IP management are critical for evaluation
- Verify country eligibility carefully, especially for China-based partners
- Check topic-specific participation restrictions in call text
- Consider [[Innovation Procurement]] (PCP/PPI) as complementary pathway
- Business model and market readiness are more heavily weighted than in RIAs

# Sources

- programme-guide_horizon_en.pdf (Sections 1, 8, 15)
- wp-1-general-introduction_horizon-2026-2027_en.pdf (p. 4)

name: AI Ecosystem in Horizon Europe
summary: This synthesis maps the full AI investment and governance landscape within [[Horizon Europe]].
body:
# AI Ecosystem in Horizon Europe

This synthesis maps the full AI investment and governance landscape within [[Horizon Europe]].

---

## AI Investment Architecture (2026-2027)

| Component | Budget |
|-----------|--------|
| [[AI in Science]] horizontal call | EUR 90 million |
| AI in science through rest of WP | EUR 775+ million |
| Total AI development investment | EUR 2,023 million |
| Digital transition (all HE, estimated) | 33% of total funds |
| AI-contributing share of clusters/RI/WPERA/EIE/Missions/NEB/Horizontal | 35.8% (EUR 3,780M) |
| Core digital technologies (HE lifetime minimum) | EUR 13 billion |

## AI Across Programme Architecture

### Horizontal Level
- **[[AI in Science]]** call: thematic networks of excellence, automated scientific discovery, RAISE doctoral networks
- RAISE pilot (Resource for AI Science in Europe)

### Cluster Level
- **[[Cluster 4 Digital Industry and Space]]**: primary implementing cluster for AI technologies
- **[[Cluster 1 Health]]**: AI for healthcare, biotechnology, drug discovery
- **[[Cluster 5 Climate Energy and Mobility]]**: AI for energy optimisation, smart mobility
- **[[Cluster 6 Food Bioeconomy Natural Resources Agriculture and Environment]]**: AI for precision agriculture
- **[[Cluster 3 Civil Security for Society]]**: AI for cybersecurity, threat detection

### Pillar I
- **[[Research Infrastructures]]**: AI-enabled research tools
- **[[European Open Science Cloud]]**: AI-ready data infrastructure

### Cross-Cutting
- **[[Ethics and Integrity]]**: AI ethics included in ethics review scope
- **[[Open Science]]**: AI models and algorithms as research outputs

## AI Governance Framework
- AI ethics is explicitly part of the Ethics Appraisal Procedure
- Guidelines on ethics by design/operational use for AI
- EU AI Act compliance expected for applicable projects
- Trustworthiness, safety, and transparency as core requirements
- Gender and intersectional bias in AI recognised as an ethical concern

## Policy Alignment
- **AI Continent Action Plan**: accelerating development and deployment
- **European Strategy for AI in Science**: RAISE pilot
- **[[Open Strategic Autonomy]]**: AI sovereignty, 'made in Europe'
- **[[Clean Industrial Deal]]**: AI for industrial decarbonisation
- **[[Competitiveness Compass]]**: AI for economic competitiveness

## Strategic Observations
1. AI is the single largest thematic investment in the 2026-2027 WP
2. The horizontal call model treats AI as a cross-domain enabler, not a standalone discipline
3. The RAISE pilot signals a shift toward AI as research infrastructure
4. Ethics governance is embedded early (ethics review) rather than post-hoc
5. Doctoral networks indicate long-term workforce investment
6. AI investment spans all three strategic orientations (green, digital, resilience)

name: Funding Instrument Comparison
summary: This synthesis compares the key funding instruments and mechanisms in [[Horizon Europe]].
body:
# Funding Instrument Comparison

This synthesis compares the key funding instruments and mechanisms in [[Horizon Europe]].

---

## Action Types

| Action Type | Purpose | Funding Rate | Key Characteristics |
|-------------|---------|-------------|---------------------|
| **Research and Innovation Actions (RIA)** | Generate new knowledge / explore feasibility | 100% | Evaluated on Excellence, Impact, Implementation |
| **Innovation Actions (IA)** | Prototyping, testing, piloting, market replication | 70% (100% for non-profit) | Closer to market; China entities excluded |
| **Coordination and Support Actions (CSA)** | Networking, coordination, policy support | 100% | No research component; supports innovation procurement feasibility |
| **Programme Co-fund Actions** | Top-up funding for national/regional programmes | Variable | Used for MSCA COFUND, ERA-NET |
| **PCP Actions** | Pre-commercial procurement of R&D services | Variable | Buyer groups (min. 2 countries); no commercial deployment |
| **PPI Actions** | Public procurement of innovative solutions | Variable | Buyer groups; near-market solutions |

## Evaluation Criteria

| Criterion | RIA/IA Weight | Key Elements |
|-----------|---------------|--------------|
| **Excellence** | Highest | Methodology, [[Open Science]], state of the art, gender dimension |
| **Impact** | High | Outcomes, pathway to impact, D&E plan, policy alignment |
| **Implementation** | Moderate | Work plan, consortium, resources, management |

## Simplification Mechanisms (2026-2027)

| Mechanism | Coverage | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| [[Lump Sum Funding]] | 50% of call budget | Simplified financial management |
| [[Two-Stage Calls]] | 41 topics | Reduced submission effort |
| Anonymised evaluation | Many first-stage proposals | Reduce bias |
| Less prescriptive topics | Across Pillar II | More applicant flexibility |
| Reduced page limits | Standard templates | Lower preparation burden |
| Open topics | Expanded in Pillar II | Pathways and methods open-ended |

## Special Funding Features

### Horizontal Calls
- **[[Clean Industrial Deal]]**: EUR 540M, industry-led, cross-sectoral
- **[[AI in Science]]**: EUR 90M, cross-cluster AI applications

### Newcomer-Friendly Topics
- Specifically designed for SMEs, start-ups, civil society, small public administrations
- Fast Track to Research and Innovation Scheme
- More financial support to third parties topics

### Key Budget Allocations (2026-2027)
- Total WP budget: >EUR 14 billion
- Climate objectives: EUR 4,903M (46.3% of relevant budget)
- Biodiversity: EUR 1,661M (15.7%)
- Digital transition: 33% of all funds
- AI investment: EUR 2,023M
- Knowledge valorisation: EUR 870M
- Critical raw materials: EUR 634M
- ERA Chairs: EUR 240M
- Clean Industrial Deal horizontal: EUR 540M
- AI in Science horizontal: EUR 90M

## Cross-Cutting Obligations

All funded projects must address:
- [[Open Science]] (mandatory practices)
- [[Ethics and Integrity]] (ethics self-assessment)
- [[Gender Equality]] (GEP eligibility + gender dimension)
- [[Dissemination and Exploitation]] (D&E plan)
- [[Do No Significant Harm]] (environmental assessment)
- Security self-assessment

name: Horizon Europe Policy Evolution 2021-2027
summary: This page tracks how Horizon Europe has evolved across its lifetime, based on the Programme Guide version history and the shift from the first to the second strategic plan.
body:
# Horizon Europe Policy Evolution 2021-2027

This page tracks how Horizon Europe has evolved across its lifetime, based on the Programme Guide version history and the shift from the first to the second strategic plan.

---

## Programme Guide Version History

| Version | Date | Key Changes |
|---------|------|-------------|
| 1.0 | 17.06.2021 | Initial version |
| 1.1-1.5 | 2021-2022 | Third country association updates (Iceland, Norway, Moldova, Turkey, Georgia, Israel, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina) |
| 2.0 | 15.04.2022 | International cooperation overhaul; added R&I Foreign Interference section; China participation restrictions introduced |
| 3.0 | 01.04.2023 | Updated introduction and international cooperation; HE calls opened to international organisations; transitional arrangements clarified |
| 4.0 | 15.10.2023 | PCP/PPI guidance updated |
| 5.0 | 15.05.2025 | Major update: Russia measures; strengthened [[Gender Equality]]; updated [[SSH Integration]]; new Part 11 [[Societal Readiness]]; updated [[Ethics and Integrity]]; updated [[Do No Significant Harm]]; updated [[Open Science]]; removed obsolete RDM plan requirements |
| 5.1 | 15.09.2025 | Updated [[Open Science]] section |

## Key Policy Shifts

### 1. From Activity-Driven to Impact-Driven (2021)
The defining paradigm shift of Horizon Europe. Work programmes organised around 'Destinations' (expected impacts) and 'Topics' (expected outcomes), replacing the activity-driven structure of Horizon 2020. This is reinforced through Key Impact Pathways tracking 9 story lines.

### 2. Geopolitical Tightening (2022-2025)
- China excluded from Innovation Actions (V2.0, 2022)
- Russia/Belarus/non-government Ukraine territories excluded from all participation (V3.0+)
- Russia-specific restrictive measures expanded via Council Regulation 2024/1745 (V5.0)
- [[Open Strategic Autonomy]] participation restrictions strengthened
- 22 countries now associated to HE

### 3. Gender Mainstreaming Deepened (2022-2025)
- GEP eligibility criterion introduced (calls from 2022)
- Gender dimension in R&I content mandatory by default
- Gender balance as ranking criterion
- EIC gender targets (40% women-led pitches, 50% women in advisory)
- Three gender categories (woman, man, non-binary)
- Intersectionality framework introduced

### 4. Simplification Acceleration (2026-2027)
- Topic descriptions shortened (-33% WP length)
- Fewer topics (-35%)
- Single-project topics reduced by 50%
- [[Lump Sum Funding]] expanded to 50% of call budget
- [[Two-Stage Calls]] expanded to 41 topics with anonymised first-stage
- Reduced proposal template complexity

### 5. Horizontal Calls Innovation (2026-2027)
- [[Clean Industrial Deal]] horizontal call (EUR 540M) — first cross-cluster industry call
- [[AI in Science]] horizontal call (EUR 90M) — first cross-cluster AI call
- Bottom-up, non-prescriptive, open topic design

### 6. Open Science Maturation (2021-2025)
- V5.0-5.1: updated open science section
- Moved beyond open access to comprehensive open science framework
- FAIR principles operationalised through [[European Open Science Cloud]]
- Research data management (RDM) mandatory; DMPs by month 6
- Removed obsolete RDM plan requirements (V5.0)

### 7. Societal Readiness Formalisation (2025)
- New Part 11 added in V5.0, recognising [[Societal Readiness]] as a distinct cross-cutting dimension alongside [[SSH Integration]] and [[Social Innovation]]
- Builds on Responsible Research and Innovation (RRI) from Horizon 2020

## Strategic Plan Transition: 2021-2024 to 2025-2027

| Dimension | First Strategic Plan (2021-2024) | Second Strategic Plan (2025-2027) |
|-----------|----------------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| Strategic orientations | 4 orientations | 3 orientations (consolidated) |
| Partnerships | Existing portfolio | 9 new partnerships announced |
| Biodiversity | General commitment | 10% budget target |
| AI | Growing emphasis | Dedicated horizontal call + EUR 2B |
| Industrial policy | Green Deal focus | Clean Industrial Deal + Competitiveness Compass |
| Talent | General mobility | Choose Europe for Science initiative |
| Simplification | Lump sums piloted | 50% lump sums, two-stage calls, shorter WP |

## Open Questions
- How will the horizontal call model evolve in future FPs?
- Will the simplification measures reduce administrative burden without compromising quality?
- How will geopolitical restrictions affect the international collaboration landscape?
- What is the long-term impact of shifting from 4 to 3 strategic orientations?

name: Horizon Europe Strategic Orientations Map
summary: This synthesis maps the three overarching strategic orientations of the [[Horizon Europe Strategic Plan 2025-2027]] to the programme's architecture and policy ecosystem.
body:
# Horizon Europe Strategic Orientations Map

This synthesis maps the three overarching strategic orientations of the [[Horizon Europe Strategic Plan 2025-2027]] to the programme's architecture and policy ecosystem.

---

## The Three Strategic Orientations

### 1. The Green Transition

**Policy drivers**: [[European Green Deal]], [[Clean Industrial Deal]], European Climate Law, Nature Restoration Regulation, European Water Resilience Strategy, European Ocean Pact

**Budget allocation**: at least 35% of HE funding to climate objectives (EUR 4,903M in 2026-2027); at least 10% to [[Biodiversity]] (EUR 1,661M)

**Primary clusters**:
| Cluster | Climate contribution |
|---------|---------------------|
| [[Cluster 5 Climate Energy and Mobility]] | 89.7% (EUR 1,644M) |
| [[Cluster 6 Food Bioeconomy Natural Resources Agriculture and Environment]] | 66.1% (EUR 778M) |
| [[Cluster 3 Civil Security for Society]] | 28.6% (EUR 96M) |
| [[Cluster 4 Digital Industry and Space]] | 22.6% (EUR 437M) |
| [[Cluster 1 Health]] | 16.6% (EUR 176M) |
| [[Cluster 2 Culture Creativity and Inclusive Society]] | 10.0% (EUR 64M) |

**Cross-cutting mechanisms**: [[Do No Significant Harm]] principle, [[EU Missions]] (Climate Adaptation, Smart Cities, Oceans, Soil), [[Circular Economy]] actions, [[New European Bauhaus]]

**Horizontal calls**: [[Clean Industrial Deal]] (EUR 540M) — decarbonisation of energy-intensive industries + clean technologies for climate action

---

### 2. The Digital Transition

**Policy drivers**: AI Continent Action Plan, European Strategy for AI in Science, Digital Decade targets, [[Competitiveness Compass]]

**Budget allocation**: 33% of all HE funds to digital transition; EUR 13B minimum to core digital technologies over HE lifetime; EUR 2,023M to AI in 2026-2027; estimated 35.8% of cluster/RI/WPERA/EIE/Missions/NEB/Horizontal funds contribute to AI

**Primary clusters**: [[Cluster 4 Digital Industry and Space]] (lead), with AI integration across all clusters

**Cross-cutting mechanisms**: [[AI in Science]] horizontal call (EUR 90M + EUR 775M across WP), [[European Open Science Cloud]], digital health, precision agriculture, smart mobility

**Key research themes**: [[Artificial Intelligence]], [[Quantum Technologies]], cybersecurity, digital twins, space technologies

---

### 3. A More Resilient, Competitive, Inclusive, and Democratic Europe

**Policy drivers**: [[Competitiveness Compass]], [[Open Strategic Autonomy]], ProtectEU Strategy, European Preparedness Union Strategy, European Economic Security Strategy

**Budget allocation**: EUR 634M to [[Critical Raw Materials]]; EUR 240M for ERA Chairs; EUR 870M for knowledge valorisation

**Primary clusters**: [[Cluster 3 Civil Security for Society]] (security), [[Cluster 2 Culture Creativity and Inclusive Society]] (democracy, inclusion), all clusters (competitiveness)

**Cross-cutting mechanisms**: [[Choose Europe for Science]], [[Widening Participation]], [[International Cooperation]], [[European Partnerships]] (9 new partnerships in strategic plan), European Startup and Scaleup Hubs

**Key policies**: [[Gender Equality]], [[SSH Integration]], [[Social Innovation]], [[Societal Readiness]]

---

## Strategic Convergence Points

Several entities sit at the intersection of multiple orientations:

1. **[[Clean Industrial Deal]]**: green transition + competitiveness + digital (clean tech + industry + AI)
2. **[[AI in Science]]**: digital transition + competitiveness + green (AI for materials, agriculture, climate)
3. **[[European Partnerships]]**: all three orientations through sectoral partnerships
4. **[[EU Missions]]**: green + resilience + digital (cities, health, climate, nature)
5. **[[Open Strategic Autonomy]]**: competitiveness + security + digital sovereignty

## Detected Strategic Narratives

- **From activity-driven to impact-driven**: paradigm shift in programme design (destinations, expected outcomes, Key Impact Pathways)
- **Growing convergence of green and digital**: AI for climate, digital twins for energy, smart agriculture
- **Horizontal calls as policy instruments**: CID and AI in Science break cluster silos
- **Simplification as strategy**: fewer topics (-35%), shorter WP (-33%), lump sums (50%), two-stage calls (41), less prescriptive descriptions
- **Talent competition**: Choose Europe for Science reflects geopolitical competition for researchers
- **Supply chain sovereignty**: CRM, advanced materials, quantum — reducing dependencies

name: Horizon Europe Cluster Interactions
summary: This synthesis maps the inter-cluster relationships and convergence points across Pillar II of [[Horizon Europe]].
body:
# Horizon Europe Cluster Interactions

This synthesis maps the inter-cluster relationships and convergence points across Pillar II of [[Horizon Europe]].

---

## Cluster Interaction Matrix

| | CL1 Health | CL2 Culture | CL3 Security | CL4 Digital | CL5 Climate | CL6 Food/Bio |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **CL1** | -- | Social determinants | Health security | Digital health, AI | Climate-health | Food safety, env. health |
| **CL2** | Health inequalities | -- | Democracy, rights | Digital inclusion | Just transition | Cultural landscapes |
| **CL3** | Health preparedness | Fundamental rights | -- | Cybersecurity | Disaster resilience | Food security threats |
| **CL4** | AI in medicine | Digital culture | Digital security | -- | Energy tech, digital twins | Precision agriculture |
| **CL5** | Climate-health | Just transition | Climate resilience | Energy/manufacturing | -- | Bioeconomy, land-use |
| **CL6** | Environmental health | Cultural heritage | Biosecurity | Digital agriculture | Biomass energy | -- |

## Cross-Cluster Convergence Themes

### 1. Artificial Intelligence
All clusters engage with AI: health diagnostics (CL1), cultural AI ethics (CL2), security AI (CL3), core AI tech (CL4), smart energy/mobility (CL5), precision agriculture (CL6). Estimated EUR 2,023M investment.

### 2. Climate Action
All clusters contribute to the 35% climate spending target, from 10% (CL2) to 89.7% (CL5).

### 3. Clean Industrial Deal
The horizontal call connects CL4 (digital/industry) and CL5 (energy/mobility) with EUR 540M, while CL6 contributes circularity perspectives.

### 4. Social Sciences and Humanities
While CL2 is the primary SSH cluster, [[SSH Integration]] is mandated across all clusters. SSH-flagged topics appear in every cluster.

### 5. Biodiversity
Primarily CL6, but with contributions from CL1 (environmental health), CL5 (land-use change), and missions.

## Horizontal Call Architecture

The 2026-2027 WP introduces horizontal calls that formally break cluster silos:

```
Horizontal Activities
├── Clean Industrial Deal (EUR 540M)
│   ├── CL4 co-financing
│   ├── CL5 co-financing
│   └── Cross-sectoral industry topics
├── AI in Science (EUR 90M)
│   ├── Cross-cluster AI applications
│   ├── RAISE doctoral networks
│   └── Thematic networks of excellence
└── New European Bauhaus
    └── Cross-cluster sustainability + arts
```

## Institutional Bridges
- **[[European Partnerships]]**: operate across clusters (e.g., automotive partnerships link CL4+CL5)
- **[[EU Missions]]**: by design cross-cluster and transdisciplinary
- **[[Joint Research Centre]]**: provides scientific support to all clusters
- **[[European Open Science Cloud]]**: data infrastructure for all clusters

## Budget Distribution (2026-2027 Climate Action)

```
CL5 Climate Energy Mobility    ████████████████████ EUR 1,644M (89.7%)
CL6 Food Bioeconomy            █████████████       EUR 778M   (66.1%)
CL4 Digital Industry Space     █████               EUR 437M   (22.6%)
CL1 Health                     ██                  EUR 176M   (16.6%)
CL3 Civil Security             █                   EUR 96M    (28.6%)
CL2 Culture Creativity         █                   EUR 64M    (10.0%)
Horizontal/Missions/NEB        ██████████████████   EUR 1,395M (68.7%)
```
